messages.content: Hi — Maya from Northwind Logistics. We just signed up for the Team plan. I run the dispatch-automation team here in Paris. We're planning to use Statewave to give our routing copilot memory across drivers and shipments.
Welcome, Maya! Glad to have Northwind on board. I've noted your use case — dispatch automation for routing — so future answers can lean on it. Anything blocking your first ingest?
We'll be in Python. Strong preference for typed SDKs — we run mypy --strict everywhere.
Got it. The Python SDK ships full Pydantic models and type stubs; mypy --strict should pass clean. I'll keep examples in Python with explicit types when answering you.
Volume question — we'll be writing roughly 500k shipment-events per month. Is that fine on the Team plan?

500k episodes/month is well within the Team plan's headroom. Recommended pattern: one subject per active driver-route, archive after 90 days. I've noted your projected volume so future capacity questions reference it.
Setting up webhooks. Our pattern at Northwind is hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/<service>/<event-type>. Is that supported?

Yes — Statewave POSTs to whatever URL you register, no path constraints. I'd suggest hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/statewave/<event-type>. Noted as your standard pattern so I won't suggest different URL shapes later.
One more — please default to async examples. Our codebase is fully async (FastAPI + asyncpg).
Noted. I'll default to AsyncStatewaveClient in any code I show you.

payload.title: SSO login broken — all users see 'invalid signature'

messages.content: Urgent — nobody at Northwind can log in. SAML SSO returns 'invalid signature' for every user.

Opening this as ticket #4821 at high severity. 'Invalid signature' across all users is almost always a signing-cert problem on the IdP side — either expired or rotated without updating us. Could you check the cert expiry on your Okta SAML app first?
Cert expired this morning. Auto-rotation didn't fire.

Confirmed cause. Rotate the cert in Okta and upload the new public cert in Account → SSO → Identity Provider. Average rotation time on a fresh cert is ~30 minutes for new sessions to clear; existing sessions stay valid.

payload.resolution: Customer rotated expired SAML signing cert in Okta and uploaded the new public cert. SSO restored ~30m after upload. No code change required.

messages.content: Login is sluggish for some users today. Not broken, just slow.

Could be SSO-adjacent again — Northwind hit a SAML cert issue last week (#4821). Checking if this is a regression: are the affected users on a specific IdP group? If only some users are slow, it's more likely a session-cache problem than SSO; if everyone is slow, I'd look at SSO first.
Only users in our 'drivers' group, actually.
Then it's almost certainly session-cache pressure on that group, not SSO. I'll save this as a distinct symptom from #4821 so we don't conflate them in future.
Where do I find the April invoice?

Account → Billing → Invoices. April will appear there on the 1st of next month. PDF download is the link on the right of each row. Sending receipts to billing@northwind-logistics.fr — your registered billing email.
Added our VAT ID for the next invoice — FR12345678901.
Confirmed and saved on the account. May invoice onwards will carry the VAT ID and reverse-charge wording for EU B2B.
Feature request: can we filter webhook deliveries to specific event types? Right now we're getting everything and filtering downstream.

Logged as a feature request and tagged on your account. Per-event-type webhook filters are on the roadmap but not committed — I won't promise a date. If it becomes a procurement blocker, ping me and we'll escalate to product.

payload.title: API timing out at 30s — context endpoint affected

messages.content: We've been seeing /v1/context time out for over four hours. Posted in the channel earlier — nobody picked up.

Apologies — the EU on-call rotation didn't catch this and it took until US hours for someone to reach you. That's a first-response SLA breach on #4937 and we'll issue a service credit. On the technical side: 30s timeouts on /v1/context point at pgvector index pressure. Can you confirm subject_id has >50k memories?
It does — one of our long-running fleet subjects.
Recommend splitting fleet subjects into per-region or per-quarter scopes; ranking is fine, but unbounded subject growth slows the vector probe. I'll send a runbook.

payload.resolution: Customer split a 60k-memory fleet subject into per-region subjects; latency returned to baseline. Service credit of one prorated month issued for first-response SLA breach (4h vs 1h target). Internal action: route Northwind high-severity tickets to EU on-call directly.

payload.author: support-lead
payload.content: Action: Northwind high-severity tickets must page EU on-call directly. They've been hit by the US-hours triage gap once already (#4937). Set up paging rule today.

messages.content: Quick check — what's the recommended way to backfill 6 months of historical shipment events into Statewave?

Use POST /v1/episodes/batch with up to 200 episodes per call, parallelized 4-wide. At your projected ~3M backfill volume that's ~6 hours. Set last_compiled_at on each batch so the compiler doesn't re-process old data on every call. Want a runnable Python async script using your subject scheme?
Yes please.
Sending an async script that uses AsyncStatewaveClient and your hooks.northwind-logistics.fr webhook pattern for progress notifications. mypy --strict clean.
We're seeing way fewer compiled memories than we expected on the dispatch-automation subjects. ~5 memories per 100 episodes.

That ratio means heuristic compilation. For structured shipment events you'll want STATEWAVE_COMPILER_TYPE=llm — typically ~2× density on operational data. Heuristic compiler under-extracts from non-conversational episode shapes.
Okay, switching to llm.
Once flipped, run a one-time recompile on existing subjects so old episodes get re-extracted. Recompile is idempotent — no duplicates.
Procurement is asking about renewal — we're tracking renewal for September. Likely expanding seats; we want to add the dock-ops team.

Renewal noted for 2026-09-15 with expansion intent (dock-ops team). I'll flag your account so account-management reaches out 60 days before renewal with the expansion quote. Anything specific you want them to prepare?
A breakdown of our SLA performance over the last quarter. Procurement will ask.
Will include first-response and resolution SLA stats, with #4937 explicitly called out and the credit shown.

payload.author: account-management
payload.content: Northwind expansion intent for renewal 2026-09-15: dock-ops team to be added. Prepare expansion quote and SLA-performance summary by 2026-07-15.

messages.content: Can you remind me what we landed on for backfill batch size?

200 episodes per call, parallelized 4-wide. We sized it against your 3M-event backfill (~6 hours). The script is the async one I sent on April 25; it uses your hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/<event-type> webhook pattern.
